description: The Red Devils Motorcycle Club is an international 1% outlaw motorcycle club and the principal support club of the Hells Angels. Founded in Karlstad, Sweden in 2001, it has chapters in nearly 20 countries. Known as the most elite and violent Hells Angels support group, it engages in drug and gun trafficking, murders, and other violent crimes. The club is often called the 'Death Patrol' in the biker scene.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Melbourne chapter Red Devils clubhouse was raided by police in 2015 after a number of bullets were fired at their Clayton South clubhouse.
country_links.context: The Red Devils MC has a chapter in Melbourne, Australia, whose clubhouse was raided and members charged with firearms offenses.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Red Devils charter would then go on to be established in Belgium in 2003
country_links.context: The Red Devils MC established a charter in Belgium in 2003.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Red Devils Motorcycle Club currently has 18 charters in Canada.
country_links.context: The Red Devils MC has 18 charters in Canada, and members were arrested in relation to organized crime, drugs, and money laundering.

country_links.evidence_quote: A charter emerged the following year in the United States in Massachusetts.
country_links.context: The Red Devils MC has charters and members in the United States, involved in drug trafficking, weapons offenses, and violent incidents.
